info.title: MSPWare Boarding API
info.description:
Collection of endpoints for creating and managing merchant boarding applications, and retrieving and updating electronic forms.



API Limits
10 requests per second and 60,000 requests per day - anything greater will return a 429 status (too many requests)

get.summary: Get a list of available application types which can be managed via the API

parameters.name: salespersonid
parameters.description: Will return the application types available to the specified salesperson ID - if not specified, will return the application types available to the calling user

get.summary: Get a list of available template applications for a given application type ID

parameters.name: merchantapplicationtypeno
parameters.description: Merchant application type ID

parameters.name: salespersonid
parameters.description: Will return the application templates available to the specified salesperson ID - if not specified, will return the application types available to the calling user

get.summary: Get the schema for an application type
get.description:
Retrieve a JSON schema that represents all of the form fields, business logic, data validation types, etc... for a merchant application type's electronic form. This is the same exact object that is used in MSPWare to generate the form UI. Each named form field can be submitted in a PUT request to a merchant application form to save responses.

Note: A very limited partial example response is shown below.

parameters.name: merchantapplicationtypeno
parameters.description: Merchant application type ID

post.summary: Create a new merchant boarding application

get.summary: Retrieve a list of merchant applications using optional filters, pagination and sorting

parameters.name: pagesize
parameters.description: Number of records to return per request

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Starting page

parameters.name: salespersonid
parameters.description: Filter by salespersonid

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: if empty returns all applications, 'submitted' returns apps submitted to elavon, 'paperwork' returns apps not yet submitted

parameters.name: apptype
parameters.description: Filter by application type. Get the list of available apptypes from GET application/types

parameters.name: ordering
parameters.description: Sort order for the query. You can use primary and secondary sort. example created_on DESC, dba ASC. Sortable columns : dba,rep,status,last_updated,type,dateadded

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Filter by tag attached to an application record. Get the list of available tags from GET application/tags

parameters.name: template
parameters.description: Filter by template name that the application was copied from. Get the list of available templates from GET application/types/:merchantapplicationtypeno/templates

parameters.name: sla
parameters.description: Filter by sla. options are Y (within SLA) or N (SLA Overdue)

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter based on a particular status. Get the list of available statuses from GET application/statuses

parameters.name: daterange
parameters.description: Filter applications by a date range starting from present back to a time. options are all, ytd, mtd, or n_datepart. example 60_d, 30_m, or 7_y

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: Used in conjunction with daterange, specify the timezone for inclusion of records in a range. GMT is default. See list of available timezone strings here: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: match a search term on App Name, MID, AWB, or Salesperson

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: optionally all filtering can be passed as json in the body instead of url parameters

put.summary: Partial data save to a merchant application electronic form
put.description:
Pass a JSON object of response to save to a form. This is a partial save, meaning only the fields passed in the request are saved to the electronic form - in other words, this is a document merge and not a document replace.

To clear a field, the field name must be explicitly sent with an empty string value.
Fields marked as 'repeatable' in the form schema must be passed as arrays (see example below)

parameters.name: merchantapplicationno
parameters.description: Merchant application ID

200.description: Form details successfully saved and returned.

example: At least one equipment or VAR selection is required.

friendly.example: 5 fields were cleared because they were hidden by form rules.

error.example: Validation errors - please see details in response object

example: At least one equipment or VAR selection is required.

friendly.example: 5 fields were cleared because they were hidden by form rules.

error.example: Validation errors - please see details in response object

get.summary: Get the schema for an application type
get.description: Get the current state of the application

parameters.name: merchantapplicationtypeno
parameters.description: Merchant application type ID

get.summary: DEPRECATED (will be removed in next version) - Use /lookups/statuses instead
Get a list of available application tags which can used for filtering applications

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: match a search term for the tag. if none supplied only top 5 tags will be returned

parameters.name: merchantapplicationno
parameters.description: Merchant application No

delete.description: A soft delete, and cancels any previous submissions

parameters.name: merchantapplicationno
parameters.description: Merchant application ID

put.summary: Promotes a merchant application to the next state in the workflow

parameters.name: merchantapplicationno
parameters.description: Merchant application ID

200.description: Promotion completed successfully and returned new state

put.summary: Demotes a merchant application to the previous state in the workflow

get.summary: Returns the current status of the merchant application

parameters.name: merchantapplicationno
parameters.description: Merchant application ID

put.description:
First validates, then submits to the processor.

NOTE: This process is async and may take as long as 4 minutes. call GET status periodically to get the current status of the submission

parameters.name: merchantapplicationno
parameters.description: Merchant application ID

get.summary: Returns a list of documents previously attached to this application

parameters.name: merchantapplicationno
parameters.description: Merchant application ID

post.summary: Uploads a single document using multipart form-data.
post.description: Add a form-data element with key set to file, value set to filename and the contents set to a bytearray. For sample code see: https://github.com/jjakob-payroc/mspware-api

parameters.name: merchantapplicationno
parameters.description: Merchant application ID

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: single file to upload, supply the filename in the entry field

get.summary: Downloads the contents of the specified document number

parameters.name: merchantapplicationno
parameters.description: Merchant application ID

parameters.name: merchantapplicationdocumentno
parameters.description: Merchant application ID

delete.summary: Deletes a document attached to the specified document number

parameters.name: merchantapplicationno
parameters.description: Merchant application ID

parameters.name: merchantapplicationdocumentno
parameters.description: Merchant application ID

post.summary: Packages the application data into a signable PDF and ships it to the e-sign provider for signatures.
post.description: Requires a 100% complete application form. Unique signing links are created for each principle defined on the application form. Does NOT send a signing request to anyone -- you can use the resulting signatures "package" to either send an email request to each principle individually or simply retrieve the signing link(s).

parameters.name: merchantapplicationno
parameters.description: Merchant application ID

parameters.name: doclist
parameters.description: Specify a comma separated list of document ids from the documents attached to the application record to include in the packaged signature document.

parameters.name: sendEmail
parameters.description: Automatically send an email with signing link to recipient(s) after the signature document is created.

get.summary: Retrieve the package of application signers, statuses, and overall state of the application signature process.

parameters.name: merchantapplicationno
parameters.description: Merchant application ID

200.description: Successfully returns the signing status for the supplied merchant application number.

get.summary: Retrieve a signing link for an individual signer on an application.
get.description: The signing link can be opened directly or embedded in an iframe, and will open a signing window served by the e-signature provider. This link should only be served to or shared with the intended signer of the application.

parameters.name: merchantapplicationno
parameters.description: Merchant application ID

parameters.name: emailAddress
parameters.description: Use the email address to reference the individual signing the application - this email must exactly match one of the emails from the application signers list.

200.description: Successfully returns the signing status for the supplied merchant application number.

get.summary: Send a secure email containing a signing link to one of the signers on your application.
get.description: The specified signer will receive an email with a link to a secure signing portal which expires after 72 hours.

parameters.name: merchantapplicationno
parameters.description: Merchant application ID

parameters.name: emailAddress
parameters.description: Use the email address to reference the individual signing the application - this email must exactly match one of the emails from the application signers list.

200.description: Successfully returns the signing status for the supplied merchant application number.

post.summary: Creates a PDF copy of the merchant application with empty signing fields.
post.description: Generates a PDF copy of the application, attaching it to the application record and returning a document ID which can be used in a followup GET request.

parameters.name: merchantapplicationno
parameters.description: Merchant application ID

post.summary: Generate and attach a Canadian Code of Conduct document to an Elavon application.
post.description: Creates a Canadian Code of Conduct document and attaches it to an Elavon application - required for submitting or sending any Elavon Canada merchant applications for signatures.

parameters.name: merchantapplicationno
parameters.description: Merchant application ID

get.summary: Validates an ABA routing number

parameters.name: routingnumber
parameters.description: Nine digit routing number. Note: Canadian format only 8 digits: 5 digit branch or transit, 3 digit bank or institution

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: if routingnumber not found, you can optionally specify the international country code US (USA) or CA (Canada) and this will clear cache for this route and cause a central lookup for new data

get.summary: Get list of notes for a merchant

parameters.name: mid
parameters.description: Required: The external system ID of the merchant

parameters.name: salesteamno
parameters.description: Optional: The salesteamno to determine accesss privileges. By default uses the salesteam associated with the apikey. can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Optional: The username to filter records by. can be present in the url or body.

parameters.name: daterange
parameters.description: Optional: Filter notes by a date range starting from present back to a time. options are all, ytd, mtd, or n_datepart. example 60_d, 30_m, or 7_y. can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: Optional: Used in conjunction with daterange, specify the timezone for inclusion of records in a range. GMT is default. See list of available timezone strings here: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones. can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: notetype
parameters.description: Optional: The type of note controlling visibility. 0 = User visible (default), 1= Visible to All, 2 = Officestaff visible. can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Optional: match a search term on Note contents

200.description: Successfully returns a list of notes for a merchant

post.summary: Add a new note to a merchant account
post.description: Pass the note params in the body as JSON object or as a url parameters.

parameters.name: mid
parameters.description: Required: The external system ID of the merchant

parameters.name: note
parameters.description: Required: note content to save. can be present in the url or body.

parameters.name: notetype
parameters.description: Optional: The type of note controlling visibility. 0 = User visible (default), 1= Visible to Everyone (public), 2 = Officestaff visible (admin). can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: salesteamno
parameters.description: Optional: The salesteamno to determine accesss privileges. Defaults to the salesteam of the apikey. can be present in the url or body.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Optional: The username for history. Defaults to the owner of the apikey. can be present in the url or body.

parameters.name: sendalert
parameters.description: Optional: alert salesteam. 0=no alerts (default), 1=alert. can be present in the url or body.

delete.summary: Delete an existing merchant note (soft delete)
delete.description: Pass the external mid as a parameter in the url.

parameters.name: mid
parameters.description: Merchant ID external

parameters.name: noteid
parameters.description: Note ID to delete, received from GET notes

parameters.name: mid
parameters.description: Optional: The external system ID of the merchant

parameters.name: salesteamno
parameters.description: Optional: The salesteamno to determine accesss privileges. By default uses the salesteam associated with the apikey. can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: daterange
parameters.description: Optional: Filter notes by a date range starting from present back to a time. options are all, ytd, mtd, or n_datepart. example 60_d, 30_m, or 7_y. can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: Optional: Used in conjunction with daterange, specify the timezone for inclusion of records in a range. GMT is default. See list of available timezone strings here: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones. can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Optional: match a search term on Note contents

200.description: Successfully returns a list of calls notes across merchants

get.summary: Get a filterable picklist of available lead tags which can be used for filtering leads

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: match a search term for the tag. if none supplied only top 5 tags will be returned

get.summary: Get a filterable picklist of available lead sources which can be used for filtering leads

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: match a search term for the source. if none supplied only top 250 sources will be returned

get.summary: Gets a filterable and paginated list of leads
get.description: API implementation of the leads page in MSPWARE. See https://mspware.com/Beta#/Referrals

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number to return while paginating. Must be between 1 and 10,000. The page value can be passed as a query parameter or in the json body.

parameters.name: pagesize
parameters.description: The number of records to return in each page while paginating. Must be between 1 and 10,000. The pagesize value can be passed as a query parameter or in the json body.

parameters.name: salesteamno
parameters.description: Optional: The salesteamno to determine accesss privileges. By default uses the salesteam associated with the apikey. can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Optional: Filter the status of the lead. Options: 0=all, allOpen, noStatus, allOpen, closed, NEW Lead. Can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: followuptime
parameters.description: Optional: Filter the followup time of the lead. Options: Numeric value -1=Past Due, 0=All, 1=Today, 1+n=Number of Days in The future. Can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: verified
parameters.description: Optional: Filter whether verified. Options: Numeric value -1=All, 0=Not Verified, 1=Verified. Can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: banksourceid
parameters.description: Optional: Filter the source bank of the lead. Options: Numeric value n = agentbank_id, or one of the defaults [all, nosource, any]. Can use the search feature to get list of sources with api /leads/sources. Can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: tagno
parameters.description: Optional: Filter by tagno attached to lead record. Get the list of available tags from GET application/tags

parameters.name: sourcecontact
parameters.description: Optional: Filter by source contact. Can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: sourceperson
parameters.description: Optional: Filter by source person. Can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: sourcephone
parameters.description: Optional: Filter by source phone. Can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: leadtype
parameters.description: Optional: Filter by lead type. options: empty=all, lead, or opporunity. Can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: timeframe
parameters.description: Optional: Filter by timeframe. options: none, past, today, tomorrow, upcoming, pastdue. Can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: orderby
parameters.description: Optional: Sort responses by fieldname. options: merchant_name,contact_phone,agentbank_name,dateadded,rep_name, rep_email, followupdate, approvedflag, contact_person. Can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: dir
parameters.description: Optional: Sort direction of orderby. options: ASC or DESC. Can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: groupby
parameters.description: Optional: Group by a fieldname. options: empty, agentbank_name, or rep_name. Can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Optional filter and pagination in the body as json or as querystring parameters

200.description: Successfully returns a list of leads, paginated

post.description: Pass the lead data as a JSON object in the body of the request, or as request parameters

parameters.name: salespersonid
parameters.description: Required: Must be larger than 1. Use /leads/salespersons for a filterable list of salespersons. The page value can be passed as a query parameter or in the json body.

parameters.name: merchantname
parameters.description: Required: Merchant name/Business Name or DBA Name. can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: contactperson
parameters.description: Required: The name of the contact person. can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: Optional: Used in conjunction with daterange, specify the timezone for inclusion of records in a range. GMT is default. See list of available timezone strings here: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones. can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: equipmentownership
parameters.description: Optional: either empty or one of [Own, Rent, Lease]. can be present in the url or body json.

get.summary: Get information about a specific lead
get.description: The leadid must be larger than 0 and passed as part of the url

put.description: Pass the lead data as a JSON object in the body of the request.

parameters.description: The internal leadid of the lead to update. The leadid was returned when the lead record was first created. It can also be found by performing a GET request with filter information.

parameters.name: leadid

post.summary: Add a new note to a lead record
post.description: Pass the note params in the body as JSON object or as a url parameters.

parameters.name: leadid
parameters.description: Required: The internal lead ID of the lead record to attached to

parameters.name: referralstatus
parameters.description: Optional: The status of the referral which controls notifications. Empty or one of [NEW Lead, Left Message, Active Status for Deactivation, Waiting on Info from Merchant,A Great Plan, Preparing Proposal]. can be present in the url or body json.

parameters.name: note
parameters.description: Required: note content to save. can be present in the url or body.

parameters.name: isprivate
parameters.description: Optional: determines if the note should be kept private. 0=public, 1=private. can be present in the url or body.

parameters.name: additionalnotificationemail
parameters.description: Optional: Notify another email address other than the salesteam associated with this lead. can be present in the url or body.

parameters.name: followupdate
parameters.description: Optional: Date to followup YYYY-MM-DD format. can be present in the url or body.
